brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is singular-perturbation calculus of variations: a second-order Modica-Mortola functional that Gamma-converges to an interfacial energy for laminate configurations in elasticity. Paper B's machinery is incremental low-rank matrix factorization for recommendation, storing preferences in a KP-tree segment tree and monotonically tightening a prediction-error bound as ratings arrive. These are unrelated processes with no role-for-role correspondence; the only faint overlap is the phrase 'phase transition' (physical, in A) versus a low-rank sketch (in B), which is not shared machinery.
